name: Madison (United States)

colors.symbolism: the field, representing the city's open civic character
Lakes Mendota and Monona, between which downtown Madison sits on an isthmus
outline of the Wisconsin State Capitol building at the centre
the dome of the State Capitol

description: A white field crossed by two light-blue diagonal bands forming an off-centre saltire, with a stylised black silhouette of the Wisconsin State Capitol — its dome topped in gold — at the centre.
history: Adopted by the Madison Common Council in 2018 to replace the city's 1962 flag, which had been retired after criticism that its central black disc and four-armed sun resembled an inverted swastika and were perceived as insensitive. The new design retains the diagonal blue bands evoking Lakes Mendota and Monona but replaces the central sun with a silhouette of the Wisconsin State Capitol.

features.symbolism: two off-centre light-blue diagonals forming a saltire on a white field, representing Lakes Mendota and Monona that flank Madison's isthmus

features.role: stylised silhouette of the Wisconsin State Capitol
features.symbolism: The Wisconsin State Capitol stands at the centre of Madison's isthmus and is the city's defining landmark; Madison is both the capital of Wisconsin and the seat of Dane County.
